title: Alphonsea philastreana

The Annonaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples. Alphonsea philastreana is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers and edible fruit. It is a relatively rare species found in the wild, primarily in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia.

intro: Alphonsea philastreana, also known as the Philippine nutmeg, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
